name: Noatun
description: Noatun, meaning "ship enclosure" or "shipyard," is the dwelling of the Norse god Njord in Asgard. It is described as a magnificent hall located by the sea, reflecting Njord's association with the sea, seafaring, wind, fishing, wealth, and crop fertility. Noatun is not just a residence; it represents Njord's dominion and power over the maritime realm. It is a place of peace, prosperity, and connection to the natural world, specifically the sea. It is often depicted as a safe harbor and a place of abundance, highlighting Njord's role as a provider and protector of seafarers. The presence of Noatun within Asgard signifies the importance of the sea to the Norse gods and the interconnectedness of the divine and natural worlds. It is a symbol of maritime strength and the blessings that the sea can bestow. The hall itself is likely imagined as grand and well-maintained, reflecting Njord's status and the wealth he controls. The location by the sea allows for easy access to the water, essential for Njord's duties and enjoyment. Noatun is a testament to the Norse people's reliance on and respect for the ocean. Furthermore, it serves as a symbolic representation of the calm waters and safe harbor that Njord provides to those who navigate the seas. The hall's existence within Asgard emphasizes the integration of the sea's power and resources into the divine realm, highlighting the importance of maritime affairs in Norse cosmology. The imagery associated with Noatun evokes feelings of serenity, abundance, and the enduring power of the ocean. Noatun is also a place of trade and exchange, given Njord’s wealth and role as a benefactor.
subtitle: Njord's Hall by the Sea

keyMyths.title: Njord's Dwelling
keyMyths.description: Noatun is primarily known as the home of Njord, the god of the sea, reflecting his connection to maritime affairs and wealth.

keyMyths.title: Njord's Influence
keyMyths.description: From Noatun, Njord holds sway over the seas, granting safe passage and prosperity to those who honor him and the sea.

sources.author: Snorri Sturluson
sources.description: Provides descriptions of Noatun and Njord's association with the sea.
sources.text: Provides descriptions of Noatun and Njord's association with the sea.
